title: Error::InvalidArg stores &'static str — can't describe runtime values
description: What the code does: Error::InvalidArg(&'static str) stores a hard-coded string literal. For user-facing error types, this means the error message cannot include the actual invalid value (e.g. 'PCR index 25 out of range 0-23'). What idiomatic Rust would do: use a structured variant like InvalidArg { msg: &'static str } already has the msg field, but since the string is 'static, runtime values cannot be included. A pattern like InvalidPcrIndex(u8) or InvalidHashLen { expected: usize, got: usize } would let callers programmatically handle the error and produce better messages. Why it matters: callers building diagnostic output must either reparse the string or silently swallow the context. The variant is #[non_exhaustive] so adding structured variants is non-breaking. This should be done before 1.0 while the API is still malleable.
notes: Requires breaking API change before 1.0: replace &'static str with structured variants (InvalidPcrIndex(u8), InvalidHashLen{expected, got}). Defer to pre-1.0 API design pass.
